lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's up with him?

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: Another gift from the sea. Found him on the shore a few days ago.

lines.speaker: Hugh
lines.text: The moon... His face was the moon...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is he going to be okay?

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: Maybe. Maybe not. Only time will tell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... What are you going to do with him?

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: He'll get the same offer they all do. We don't need mouths to feed if they won't contribute. Once he's up, he can either stick around or ship out, but if he's staying, he has to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Help with what?

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: Salvage of course. We don't have the luxuries of Fortis out here. We have to make do with what we can find. Luckily for us, there's always plenty to be found thanks to all the wrecks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well I hope he gets well soon.

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: You've returned. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is he doing any better?

lines.speaker: Hugh
lines.text: The moon... His face was the moon...

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: What do you think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happens if he doesn't recover?

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: The sea can have him back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Harsh.

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: I told you before, we don't have the luxuries of Fortis out here. The dead are not useful, nor are they worth anything.

lines.speaker: Izel
lines.text: If he does live, he will have an opportunity. If he desires, he can have a home here, provided he's willing to contribute.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well I hope he gets well soon.
